items.title: Mini: Zero-shot slogan
items.prompt: Create a one-sentence product slogan for [PRODUCT].

items.title: Mini: Few-shot slogans
items.prompt:
Examples:
"[EXAMPLE 1]"
"[EXAMPLE 2]"
Create 3 more slogans in the same style for [PRODUCT].

items.title: Mini: Chain of thought
items.prompt:
Identify 3 main problems related to [SITUATION / PRODUCT / PROCESS].
Suggest solutions.
Outline a short action plan.

items.title: Mini: Prompt sequence
items.prompt:
Create 5 main theses on [TOPIC].
Outline the plan structure.
Write a 1-paragraph intro.

items.title: Mini: Instruction-giving
items.prompt:
Write a [WORD COUNT] word text on [TOPIC].
Audience: [AUDIENCE].
Tone: [TONE].
Language: [LANGUAGE].

items.title: Mini: Constraints
items.goal: Set limits and what to avoid.
items.prompt:
Task: [DESCRIBE TASK].
Constraints:
Length: [E.G. Max 200 words / 1 page].
Avoid: [E.G. jargon, ads, subjective claims].
Format: [E.G. bullets, table, paragraphs].
Language: [LANGUAGE].

items.title: Daily summary
items.prompt:
META: You are [ROLE].
INPUT:
Today's work: [ENTER]
Results: [ENTER]
Blockers: [ENTER]
Tomorrow's plan: [ENTER]
OUTPUT:
5 bullet list
1–2 sentences each

items.title: Meeting notes
items.prompt:
META: You are a meeting secretary.
INPUT:
Topic: [TOPIC]
Attendees: [NAMES]
Notes: [TEXT]
OUTPUT:
Summary
Decisions
Actions (who / when)
Open questions

items.title: Email reply
items.prompt:
META: You are a customer support specialist.
INPUT: Incoming email: [ENTER]
OUTPUT:
4–6 sentences
Professional, polite tone
Clear next step

items.title: Task list
items.prompt:
META: You are a personal work assistant.
INPUT: Text with ideas or requirements: [TEXT]
OUTPUT:
Task list
Priorities A / B / C
Deadlines if mentioned

items.title: Decision brief
items.prompt:
META: You are a business analyst.
INPUT:
Situation: [DESCRIPTION]
Options: [OPTIONS]
Data: [NUMBERS / FACTS]
OUTPUT:
Problem
2–3 options
Recommendation
Risks

items.title: Process outline
items.prompt:
META: You are a process specialist.
INPUT:
Topic: [PROCESS]
Current steps: [STEPS]
OUTPUT:
6–10 clear steps
Short sentences, action verbs

items.title: Text simplification
items.prompt:
META: You are an editor.
INPUT:
Text: [TEXT]
Audience: [AUDIENCE]
OUTPUT:
30–50% shorter version
Clear, plain language

items.title: SWOT analysis
items.prompt:
META: You are a business consultant.
INPUT:
Project / company: [DESCRIPTION]
Data: [FACTS]
OUTPUT:
SWOT table
3–5 concrete points per section

items.title: Prompt quality check (by 5 principles)
items.goal: Paste your prompt and run. Checks against 5 principles, suitable for RAG and agents.
items.prompt:
ROLE:
You are an experienced prompt engineer and training facilitator.
Your goal is to critically evaluate prompt quality and suggest improvements.

TASK:
Evaluate the given prompt against 5 principles:
1) Clarity
2) Experimentation
3) From simple to complex
4) Context
5) Word choice

INPUT:
[prompt provided]

EVALUATION FORMAT:
1. Evaluation table (1–5 score per principle)
2. Brief explanation of WHY that score
3. 3 main weak points
4. Suggested improved prompt version

RULES:
- Be specific, no generic phrases
- If information is missing – state it clearly
- If prompt is too complex – suggest a simpler V1
- Don’t rewrite unnecessarily – only fix what adds real value

OUTPUT:
Structured, clear, practically applicable response.
